symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic diarrhoea (3 months) acutely worse with visible blood and mucus
symptoms.onset: insidious over 3 months, acute deterioration over 48 hours
symptoms.character: watery stool with fresh blood and mucus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic bloody diarrhoea with mucus is a hallmark of active colonic inflammation.
Bloody diarrhoea is also infective — requires stool studies to exclude, especially superimposed C. difficile after loperamide.
Rectal bleeding is never a feature of functional bowel disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Crampy lower abdominal pain relieved by defecation, with tenesmus
symptoms.character: colicky, builds before defecation, eases after; constant painful urge to pass stool

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tenesmus and defecation-related cramping indicate rectosigmoid inflammation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss of about 6 kg

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Weight loss reflects chronicity and disease burden.
Weight loss with rectal bleeding is a gastrointestinal alarm combination mandating investigation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Subjective fever, palpitations and profound fatigue (systemic toxicity)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic features convert a colitis flare into a SEVERE attack and raise toxic-megacolon concern.
Postural symptoms indicate significant fluid/blood loss from ongoing diarrhoea and bleeding.
Objective systemic toxicity and volume depletion — a clinician-measured vital the AI Doctor infers only from reported palpitations and postural symptoms.
A Truelove and Witts systemic criterion; measured by the clinician, only reported subjectively by the patient.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse lower-quadrant abdominal tenderness with active bowel sounds, no guarding/rigidity/mass

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of guarding/rigidity argues against current peritonism, but the AI cannot perform this examination and cannot exclude progression.
Serial abdominal examination and imaging are required to exclude an evolving toxic megacolon.

investigation_details.expected_result: Markedly elevated, indicating active mucosal inflammation
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Inflammatory bowel disease activity not objectively distinguished from a functional or infective cause.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distinguishes inflammatory from functional bowel disease; never obtainable in real time via telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Continuous mucosal inflammation and ulceration confirming colitis and allowing subtyping
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The diagnosis cannot be confirmed or subtyped; deferred to specialist and not performed during an acute severe attack with megacolon risk.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Endoscopic and histological confirmation is the diagnostic gold standard — impossible via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Raised inflammatory markers and anaemia (FBC, CRP)

investigation_details.expected_result: Raised CRP with anaemia and possible leucocytosis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity and blood loss not quantified; a Truelove and Witts criterion cannot be assessed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective markers of systemic inflammation and blood loss — venous bloods, unobtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stool microscopy/culture and C. difficile toxin

investigation_details.investigation_name: Stool MCS and C. difficile toxin
investigation_details.expected_result: Required to exclude infective and C. difficile colitis before/alongside immunosuppression
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A treatable or immunosuppression-contraindicating infection could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mandatory to exclude a superimposed infection, particularly C. difficile after recent anti-motility use.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute severe colitis with systemic toxicity
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Frequent bloody diarrhoea with tenesmus, weight loss and systemic features (fever, tachycardia) defines a SEVERE attack requiring hospital-level care and exclusion of toxic megacolon.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify inflammation, anaemia, blood loss and dehydration; needed to grade severity and guide IV fluids.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Stool MCS and C. difficile toxin
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude infective/C. difficile colitis before or alongside immunosuppression.
Objective marker of mucosal inflammation to distinguish inflammatory from functional disease.
Assess for colonic dilatation (toxic megacolon) in a systemically-toxic patient with recent anti-motility use.
Specialist-led confirmation and subtyping once the acute severe attack is stabilised — deferred, not first-line during an acute megacolon-risk phase.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Anti-motility agents (e.g. loperamide) to control the diarrhoea
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Contraindicated in active colitis — slows colonic transit and can precipitate toxic megacolon.
